elements.title: Tool, Plugin & Agent Data Exchange Risks
Data Governance, Lifecycle & Classification for AI Systems
Data Availability & Resilience Failures in AI Pipelines
Disinformation & Integrity Attacks via Data Poisoning

notes:
All 21 IDs and titles confirmed directly against the authoritative PDF ("OWASP GenAI Data Security Risks & Mitigations 2026," Version 1.0 / March 2026) retrieved from the pinned source URL's download endpoint (https://genai.owasp.org/download/53429/, 103 pages). Titles taken verbatim from the PDF Table of Contents and per-entry section headings (em-dash "—" separator in source; the em-dash and ID prefix are stripped here, ampersands preserved). Three ToC lines wrapped and were resolved from the body headings: DSGAI07 "...for AI Systems", DSGAI10 "...Transformation Pitfalls", DSGAI12 "...(LLM-to-SQL/Graph)".

Cross-check passed on numeric ID against two OWASP-affiliated crosswalk sources, both carrying DSGAI01-DSGAI21 entry JSONs: the official OWASP org repo GenAI-Security-Project/GenAI-Data-Security-Initiative (crosswalk/data/entries/, source_list "DSGAI-2026") and the contributor repo emmanuelgjr/GenAI-Security-Crosswalk (data/entries/). Both map 1:1 to the PDF on numeric ID. Their machine-readable "name" fields use normalized wording (ampersand rendered as "and", parentheticals dropped, and British "Anonymisation" in the emmanuelgjr repo) and should NOT be treated as canonical titles; map on the numeric ID as instructed. The official org repo README reproduces the fuller canonical titles matching the PDF exactly.

Caveat / source inconsistency to carry downstream: the PDF's root-cause narrative prose (not the ToC or headings) contains stray references to a wider numbering — "DSGAI01–DSGAI25", "DSGAI19, DSGAI21, DSGAI25", and "DSGAI26 (Disinformation & Integrity Attacks via Data Poisoning)" — an apparent leftover from an expanded draft numbering. The authoritative enumeration is unambiguously 21 entries (DSGAI01-DSGAI21) per the ToC and section headings; disinformation is canonically DSGAI21, not DSGAI26. Ignore the stray DSGAI22-DSGAI26 prose tokens when mapping.
